title: SQL Injection in Login
status_code: 5000: 302 redirect after bypass, 5001: 200 login page with invalid credentials
vulnerable_behavior: The vulnerable app builds a SQL query using raw user-controlled username and password input, allowing authentication logic to be bypassed in the local lab.
secure_behavior: The secure app uses a parameterized query and password hash verification, so the same input is rejected.
evidence_note: Compare the login behavior using a controlled SQL injection test string in the local lab only.
root_cause: The vulnerable implementation concatenates user input directly into a SQL query string.
impact: An attacker could bypass authentication or access another account if this pattern existed in a real application.
remediation: Use parameterized queries for database access and verify passwords with a secure password hashing function.
retest_notes: Retest confirmed that the secure app rejects the same input and keeps the user unauthenticated.
